findings.description: The `isValidSearchCondition` private static method is called within `validateForSearch` in the code example, but its implementation body is not shown. The source defines the method with the logic: `return loginId.length() > 0 || kanjiName.length() > 0 || kanaName.length() > 0 || ugroupId.length() > 0 || userIdLocked.length() > 0;`. An AI reading the knowledge file code will see a call to an undefined method.
findings.source_evidence: private static boolean isValidSearchCondition(String loginId, String kanjiName, String kanaName, String ugroupId, String userIdLocked) { return loginId.length() > 0 || kanjiName.length() > 0 || kanaName.length() > 0 || ugroupId.length() > 0 || userIdLocked.length() > 0; }

findings.description: The order of setter methods in the UserEntity code example is reversed compared to the source. The knowledge file shows `setName` before `setId`, but the source defines `setId` before `setName`. The comment in the source (`// 【説明】元の表示名は"ユーザ名"`) is on `setName` to highlight it as the property being overridden, but the source's actual method order is `setId` → `setName` → `setRemarks`.
findings.source_evidence: Source UserEntity order: setId (with @PropertyName("ID")), then setName (with @PropertyName("ユーザ名")), then setRemarks. Knowledge file order: setName, then setId, then setRemarks.
